message:
perf(indexer): derive semantic artifact fingerprint and count from the same storage handle

`publish_direct_semantic_artifact` previously took both a `&FrankenStorage`
and a `&Path` to the database file. It then called
`count_total_conversations_exact(storage)` to get the conversation
count, and separately called `lexical_storage_fingerprint_for_db(db_path)`
which re-opens the DB to compute the `content-v1:N:M:K` fingerprint
embedded into the manifest. The fingerprint is constructed from the
same conversation count, so the two values had to agree exactly.

Two issues:

1. Reopening the DB just to compute the fingerprint is unnecessary work,
   especially on the publish hot path that runs after every index build.
2. More importantly: between the `count_total_conversations_exact` call
   and the `lexical_storage_fingerprint_for_db` call, another writer
   (e.g. a parallel indexer) could insert or delete a conversation,
   leaving the manifest's `conversation_count` field disagreeing by one
   with the count baked into `db_fingerprint`. Downstream consumers
   compare these to decide whether the on-disk index matches the DB,
   so even a one-row drift can cause spurious "stale artifact" rebuilds.

Switches the helper to call
`lexical_rebuild_content_fingerprint(storage, total_conversations_raw)`,
which builds the fingerprint from the same in-memory conversation count
(`count_total_conversations_exact(storage)`). The two values are now
guaranteed to come from the same logical snapshot.

The `db_path` parameter is removed from both
`publish_direct_semantic_artifact` and the only call site in `run_index`.
